stories.headline: Kalshi launches crypto perpetual futures today under codename 'Timeless'
stories.tldr: Prediction-market giant Kalshi rolls out perp futures on a handful of cryptocurrencies today, marking its first true derivatives push beyond event contracts.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A CFTC-regulated venue trading crypto perps onshore opens an institutional channel that has been offshore-only for years.
the_details: Kalshi confirmed April 27 as launch day for the product codenamed 'Timeless,' per The Information.
Perpetuals will be tied to a handful of cryptocurrencies, joining its event-contract platform.
Move follows the broader race by Robinhood and Coinbase to bring perps to US retail under CFTC rules.

stories.headline: SEC and CFTC sign historic MOU clarifying federal securities law for crypto
stories.tldr: The SEC and CFTC inked their first formal memorandum of understanding to coordinate oversight and issued joint guidance on crypto-asset securities.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Years of agency turf wars are finally being papered over, giving issuers and exchanges a clearer rulebook to operate under.
the_details: SEC Press Release 2026-26 announces the historic MOU between the two agencies.
A separate joint statement clarifies how federal securities laws apply to crypto assets.
Lands as 100+ crypto firms press the Senate to mark up the Clarity Act on market structure.

stories.headline: April 2026 turns crypto's worst hack month since Bybit, $606M lost in 18 days
stories.tldr: April losses have already topped $606M, driven by the $292M Kelp DAO and $285M Drift exploits, both attributed to North Korea's Lazarus Group.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Two Lazarus-linked breaches in three weeks reset the security baseline DeFi must hit before institutional money returns at scale.
the_details: Drift Protocol drained for $285M on April 1; Kelp DAO bridge exploited for $292M on April 19.
Together the two hits represent roughly 95% of April's reported crypto losses.
DeFi total value locked dropped from $26.4B on April 18 to ~$20B amid panic withdrawals.

stories.headline: SEC carves broker-dealer exception for DeFi UI providers in major opening
stories.tldr: The SEC's Division of Trading and Markets says certain crypto interface operators can run without broker-dealer registration under specified conditions.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Frontends for permissionless DeFi just got a regulatory off-ramp from the legal cloud that has hung over US developers for years.
the_details: April 13 staff statement names 'Covered User Interface Providers' as qualifying for non-objection.
Applies to interfaces helping users prepare and submit crypto-asset securities transactions.
Pairs with the SEC-CFTC MOU as the latest pro-crypto policy shift out of Washington.

stories.headline: USDT supply nears $150B as Bitcoin tracks best month in a year
stories.tldr: Tether's USDT stablecoin supply has surged to nearly $150 billion, deepening crypto market liquidity as BTC posts a 13.6% April gain.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Stablecoin supply is the cleanest leading indicator of dry powder — record USDT issuance signals fresh capital, not just rotation.
the_details: USDT supply added roughly $5B in recent weeks per CoinDesk reporting.
Bitcoin is up ~13.6% in April, on pace for its strongest monthly performance in a year.
BTC's 30-day correlation with the US Dollar Index hit -0.90, the most negative since 2022.
